OPPOSITION TO MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING TO RESPOND TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES (TRANSACTION ID # 58877897) FILED BY RESPONDENT BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING

DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFFS GEORGE MANDE AND RAQUEL FERRER'S RE-NOTICE OF MOTION FOR ORDER COMPELLING RESPONDENT BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING TO RESPOND TO PETITIONERS SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES IS DENIED. PETITIONERS FAILED TO SUBMIT A SEPARATE STATEMENT OF OBJECTIONS, AS REQUIRED UNDER CRC 3.1345(C). PETITIONERS ALSO HAVE NOT SHOWN HOW THE INFORMATION SOUGHT IS REASONABLY CALCULATED TO LEAD TO THE DISCOVERY OF ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE IN LIGHT OF CCP 1094.5. MOREOVER, MOST IF NOT ALL OF THE SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES RELATE TO PETITIONER MANDE AND, ON APRIL 18, 2016, AFTER THE INSTANT MOTION WAS FILED, THE COURT SUSTAINED A DEMURRER MR. MANDE'S PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE PRO TEM: AARON MINNIS, CLERK: CYNTHIA HERBERT; NOT REPORTED. =302/JPT

ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). THE COURT HAS COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING AND REPLY PAPERS. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. LAW AND MOTION 302, RESPONDENT BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE IS OVERRULED IN ITS ENTIRETY WITHOUT PREJUDICE. RESPONDENT'S REPLY BRIEF CONCEDES THAT PETITIONER FERRER'S PETITION IS TIMELY. WITH REGARD TO PETITIONER MANDE, THE COURT CANNOT DETERMINE WHETHER HIS PETITION IS ALSO TIMELY. RESPONDENT IS RAISING FACTUAL ISSUES THAT ARE OUTSIDE THE FOUR CORNERS OF THE PETITION. THE PETITION DOES NOT FACIALLY DISCLOSE THAT MR. MANDE'S ACTION IS TIME BARRED AND RESPONDENT NEEDS TO PRESENT EVIDENCE TO ESTABLISH THE STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS DEFENSE. COUNSEL FOR THE DEFENDANT IS TO PROVIDE A FORM OF ORDER AND COMPLY WITH CRC 3.1312. JUDGE: ERNEST GOLDSMITH. CLERK: ROSIE NOGUERA. REPORTER: SHERRY SAWYER, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

calendar.matters:
DEMURRER TO AMENDED PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Dec-06-2016 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Errol Zshornack telephone number 415-412-7479 for Petitioner Raquel Ferrer.
Charlotte Sachson of the Attorney General's Office telephone number 415-703-5558 for Respondent Board of Registered Nursing.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court confirmed its tentative ruling.
Respondent Board of Registered Nursing's demurrer to the first amended petition for writ of administrative mandate as to petitioner Raquel Tolentino only is sustained without leave to amend. Ms. Tolentino's writ is entirely premised on the allegation that the Board lacked jurisdiction to impose the discipline it imposed against her. Per Business & Professions Code 2761, the Board may take disciplinary action against a licensed nurse if any one grounds for discipline exists. Among those grounds is that a nurse has been convicted of practicing medicine without a license. However, nothing in section 2761 states or suggests that a nurse may not be disciplined based on facts that constitute or amount to the unlawful practice of medicine even though the nurse has not suffered a conviction for unlawful practice of medicine. Given the breadth and purpose of section 2761, such a reading is unreasonable as a matter of law. Section 2761(a) begins with the language: "unprofessional conduct . . . includes, but is not limited to" before listing several examples. This list is not exhaustive and does not preclude discipline for facts that constitute or amount to unlawful practice of medicine. Leave to amend is denied because it does not appear that petitioner can allege any facts that state a legally cognizable claim that the discipline imposed against her must be set aside. Order signed in open Court. If in the next few days Mr. Zshornack studies the case and comes up with a new basis not dependant of and not reliant on the argument about there must be a conviction, he can speak with Counsel for the Board to see if they can agree on filing of an amended complaint. If not, Mr. Zshornack needs to file a motion which the Court will allow him to do without having to comply with CCP 1005 with respect to a motion for reconsideration.

Judge: Harold Kahn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/HK)

Re-Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Respondent Board Of Registered Nursing To Respond To Petitioners Special Interrogatories
Off Calendar Apr-29-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. RODEL RODIS, APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHAR SACHSON, DEPUTY ATTORNEY GENERAL, TEL. NO. 1-415-703-5558 APPEARING FOR THE RESPONDENT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The court adopted its tentative ruling and ruled as follows: PLAINTIFFS GEORGE MANDE AND RAQUEL FERRER'S Re-Notice Of Motion For Order Compelling Respondent Board Of Registered Nursing To Respond To Petitioners Special Interrogatories is DENIED. Petitioners failed to submit a separate statement of objections, as required under CRC 3.1345(c). Petitioners also have not shown how the information sought is reasonably calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible evidence in light of CCP 1094.5. Moreover, most if not all of the special interrogatories relate to Petitioner Mande and, on April 18, 2016, after the instant motion was filed, the court sustained a demurrer Mr. Mande's Petition for Writ of Mandate without leave to amend. Order signed in open court. Judge Pro tem: Aaron Minnis, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Not Reported. =302/jpt

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Attorney Rodel Rodis telephone number 415-334-7800 for Petitioners George Mande and Raquel Ferrer.
Char Sachson of Office of the Attorney General telephone number 415-703-5558 for Respondent Board of Registered Nursing.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, Mr. Rodel's objection is overruled and RESPONDENT BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is sustained. Order signed in open Court.

Off Calendar Apr-18-2016 Text Ruling

DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION
Off Calendar Jan-27-2016 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Errol Zshornack of Law of Errol J. Zshornack telephone number 415-412-7479 specially appearing for Petitioners.
Char Sachson Deputy Attorney General telephone number 415-703-5558 for Respondent Board of Registered Nursing.

The matter was argued. Upon conclusion of argument, the Court modified its tentative ruling. RESPONDENT BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING'S DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE is overruled in its entirety without prejudice. Respondent's reply brief concedes that Petitioner Ferrer's petition is timely. With regard to Petitioner Mande, the court cannot determine whether his petition is also timely. Respondent is raising factual issues that are outside the four corners of the petition. The petition does not facially disclose that Mr. Mande's action is time barred and respondent needs to present evidence to establish the statute of limitations defense. Counsel for the Defendant is to provide a form of order and comply with CRC 3.1312.

Judge: Ernest Goldsmith. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Reporter: Sherry Sawyer, CSR #5976. (302/EG)

Off Calendar Jan-12-2016 Continued To Jan-27-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

RESPONDENT BOARD OF REGISTERED NURSING's DEMURRER TO PETITION FOR WRIT OF MANDATE/ PROHIBITION/ CERTIFICATION is continued to January 27, 2016 at 9:30 a.m., in Dept. 302 per agreement of the parties, to give the Plaintiff the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.7B. Plaintiff must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than January 12, 2016, at 4:00 pm. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). The court has courtesy copies of the moving and reply papers.
